Motion For Determination That Posting Jury Fees Is A General Appearance And Entry Of Default And/Or In The Alternative, Plaintiffs Application For An Order Allowing Service Of Summons By Publication; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Oct-22-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF CARISSA COPELAND'S Motion For Determination That Posting Jury Fees Is A General Appearance And Entry Of Default And/Or In The Alternative, Plaintiffs Application For An Order Allowing Service Of Summons By Publication is taken off calendar at the request of the moving party. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EHG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-28-2015 Set For Order To Show Cause On Nov-17-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-27-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Lyudmila F. Logvin, of Law Offices of Leonard S. Becker, Tel. 510.886.1996, for plaintiffs;
David L. Barch, of Law Offices of Gregory V. DeBernard, Tel. 925.977.1400 ext. 999, for defendants, appearing via CourtCall.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT JOHN GILMOUR, SARAH LOVE'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint is granted. To effectuate substitute service: a copy of the summons and complaint must be left at the individual's dwelling house ("usual place of abode") with a "competent member of the household" or person "apparently in charge of his or her office, place of business, or usual mailing address," at least 18 years old, who must be told what the papers are. [See CCP 415.20(b).] The person with whom the papers are left need not be a member of the family. [See Bein v. Brechtel-Jochim Group, Inc. (1992) 6 CA4th 1387, 1393.] Here, it is undisputed that Plaintiff served the summons and complaint on Mr. Joshua Wagner, at 650 Alhambra Street in Crockett, CA, on January 18, 2015. However, 650 Alhambra Street is not Defendant's dwelling house under CCP 415.20(b). Therefore the Court finds the substitute service to be invalid. Counsel may argue for costs if the Richmond address is determined to be a valid address. Order signed in open court. Judge Suzanne R. Bolanos; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/SRB)
